causal_question: What is the effect of a state adopting a Castle Doctrine (stand-your-ground) law on the logged homicide rate (ln homicide per 100,000) in that state over 2000-2010, compared with other states that did not adopt the law during this period?
vague_question: Did homicide rates in a state rise or fall after that state passed a Castle Doctrine/stand-your-ground law, relative to similar states that did not pass such a law?
